summary: Problem 6 asks whether a universal constant c exists characterizing ε-light vertex subsets in graphs, an open research-level question posed by Daniel Spielman with conjectured tight constant c=1/2. The current best proof attempt is internally inconsistent: 15 sub-issues have been individually marked resolved, yet the best-proof record reports 100 open issues and the overall solvability is estimated at only 18%, indicating the proof is far from complete despite substantial local repairs. The work is dominated by barrier-method (interlacing-polynomial style) machinery whose individual lemmas have been patched, but the global argument and the existence of the universal constant remain unestablished.
problems: Core mathematical blocker: no proof that a universal constant c (conjectured 1/2) actually exists — the central claim of the problem is unresolved, hence 18% solvability.
Status inconsistency: 15 issues marked 'resolved' while the best-proof record simultaneously lists 100 open issues, so resolution claims are not validated against the assembled global proof.

Barrier-method core is fragile: multiple Step 4 issues (Tr_sigma vs Tr equality, undefined negative pseudo-powers, Ky Fan maximum vs subadditivity, symmetrized convention used before definition) were patched piecemeal rather than re-derived from a single consistent framework.

Leverage-score / greedy induction foundation was weak: base case for arbitrary initial vertex and the lem:greedy-to-lem:bu connection were missing and only recently assembled, so the inductive backbone needs end-to-end verification.

Spectral foundations (Foster's identity, rank L = n - #components, ker L ⊆ ker L_S reduction, L_{S,V\S} ⪯ L) were only proved late and for restricted cases (e.g., connected G), risking residual gaps for disconnected graphs.
Many fixes rely on solver-agent self-reports without independent critic re-verification, so 'resolved' labels may mask reintroduced inconsistencies.
highlights: Substantial targeted progress: 15 distinct technical gaps in the barrier-update and Step-4 decomposition machinery have been individually identified and patched.
Foundational reduction (opnorm-form) and the ker L ⊆ ker L_S equivalence were explicitly rewritten, strengthening the basis of the spectral argument.
Foster's identity / rank formula was generalized beyond connected graphs, removing a connectivity restriction.
Barrier Update Lemma (lem:bu) was rewritten into 8 explicit steps closing three named gaps, and a formal proof of the Greedy Lemma was assembled, improving rigor of the inductive core.
Ky Fan maximum principle is now cited and proved separately from subadditivity, and the symmetrized Tr_sigma convention is now defined before first use.

suggested_todos.title: Reconcile the 100-open-issues best-proof record against the 15 'resolved' sub-issues; produce a single authoritative gap ledger
Perform an end-to-end critic re-verification of the assembled proof to confirm patched lemmas (Step 4, lem:bu, lem:greedy) compose into a consistent global argument
Assess feasibility of proving existence of the universal constant c (and the c=1/2 conjecture); document whether current barrier machinery can yield any explicit constant
Verify the leverage-invariant induction (base case for arbitrary v0 and greedy-to-barrier-update link) holds across all graph classes including disconnected G
Consolidate Step-4 Tr_sigma symmetrization, negative-pseudo-power, and Ky Fan usage into one rigorously defined framework rather than separate patches
Re-confirm spectral foundations (Foster's identity, rank formula, L_{S,V\S} ⪯ L) for the general disconnected case used downstream
